context_blocks.title: Runtime context 1
context_blocks.text: ok so i have all the cli worker

runtime_understanding.runtime_summary: This repository is a Python-based documentation automation service (named AutoDocX in the code) with two runtime components observed: a FastAPI backend (app.main / app.api.project_routes) served with Uvicorn on port 8000, and a Streamlit UI (ui/streamlit_app.py) served on port 8501. The backend scans local Python projects, stores scan results and LLM-generated ‘understandings’, compares scans, and builds/saves static HTML developer documentation. The system supports uploading ordered runtime/context blocks and screenshots; an optional vision-capable LLM analyzes screenshots together with code context. Storage appears to be local filesystem-based under a workspace directory (scans, understandings, documents).

tooling_stack.purpose: Primary implementation language and runtime for backend and UI
tooling_stack.evidence: Project contains many .py files (app/*, ui/streamlit_app.py) and Python-specific constructs (BaseModel, FastAPI router, Uvicorn start).

tooling_stack.purpose: Backend HTTP API that exposes endpoints for scanning, comparing, generating documentation, uploading runtime context, and LLM interactions
tooling_stack.evidence: app.api.project_routes defines many router endpoints (e.g. POST /api/projects/documentation/sync, POST /api/projects/documentation/sync-with-context), handlers use response_model and status codes consistent with FastAPI.

tooling_stack.purpose: ASGI server used to run the FastAPI backend in development
tooling_stack.evidence: Screenshot logs show command 'uvicorn app.main:app --reload' and info lines 'Uvicorn running on http://127.0.0.1:8000'.

tooling_stack.purpose: Local GUI (developer-facing) used to call backend endpoints and build multipart runtime/context payloads including screenshots
tooling_stack.evidence: ui/streamlit_app.py exists and screenshot shows the command 'streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py' starting on 0.0.0.0:8501; the streamlit module contains functions like sync_documentation_with_context and build_context_blocks_payload.

tooling_stack.purpose: Persistent storage of project scans, generated understandings, and generated HTML documentation
tooling_stack.evidence: Services document_storage, scan_storage and understanding_storage docstrings reference saving to 'workspace/scans/<project_name>/<scan_id>.json' and workspace/understandings/<project_name>/<understanding_id>.json. app.core.config.create_required_directories creates WORKSPACE_PATH, SCANS_PATH, UNDERSTANDINGS_PATH, DOCUMENTS_PATH.

tooling_stack.purpose: Generate narrative project understanding and (optionally) analyze runtime screenshots using vision-capable models
tooling_stack.evidence: app.core.config has get_llm_provider/get_llm_api_key/get_llm_model. app.services.llm contains OpenAIProvider and AnthropicProvider classes and a factory. project_understanding_service.generate_understanding and runtime_context_analyzer.analyze are called from routes.

tooling_stack.purpose: Used by the Streamlit UI to call the FastAPI backend endpoints
tooling_stack.evidence: ui/streamlit_app.py functions parse_json_response, sync_documentation use requests.post and requests.get.

runtime_flow.title: Start backend API
runtime_flow.description: Developer runs the FastAPI app with Uvicorn (typically using the command 'uvicorn app.main:app --reload'). Uvicorn serves the API at 127.0.0.1:8000 by default in the screenshots. The backend registers multiple project-related endpoints (scan, compare, understand, generate document, sync-with-context).
runtime_flow.evidence: app.main contains FastAPI app routes and app.api.project_routes defines endpoints. Screenshot 2 shows 'uvicorn app.main:app --reload' and 'Uvicorn running on http://127.0.0.1:8000' plus GET logs for /api/projects/*.

runtime_flow.title: Start Streamlit UI (optional developer GUI)
runtime_flow.description: Developer runs the Streamlit application to provide a GUI for calling the backend and uploading runtime/context blocks with screenshots. Streamlit serves on port 8501 and builds multipart requests (files + JSON) for the endpoint that accepts context and screenshots.
runtime_flow.evidence: ui/streamlit_app.py contains functions build_context_blocks_payload and sync_documentation_with_context; screenshot 1 shows 'streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py' started with Local URL: http://localhost:8501.

runtime_flow.title: Scan project or sync documentation (API call)
runtime_flow.description: A call to POST /api/projects/documentation/sync or /api/projects/documentation/sync-with-context triggers a project scan (ProjectScanner) which parses Python files and computes project-level stats and relationships, then save the scan to scan storage. If runtime context blocks and screenshots were provided, they are saved/prepared and merged with previous context.
runtime_flow.evidence: project_routes.sync_project_documentation and sync_project_documentation_with_context call project_scanner.scan_project and scan_storage.save_scan. ui/streamlit_app.sync_documentation_with_context builds multipart payloads and posts to the sync-with-context endpoint.

runtime_flow.title: Compare scans and decide action
runtime_flow.description: If a previous document exists, the backend retrieves the previous scan and uses ScanComparator.compare_scans to produce a comparison. If changes are detected (or runtime context changed), the backend generates a new understanding and document; otherwise it returns the unchanged document metadata.
runtime_flow.evidence: sync_project_documentation/sync-with-context docstrings and code call document_storage.list_documents, scan_storage.get_scan, scan_comparator.compare_scans and then either _generate_and_save_understanding/_generate_and_save_document or return unchanged response.

runtime_flow.title: Generate understanding via LLM provider
runtime_flow.description: When needed the system builds an LLM-ready project context (ProjectUnderstandingService). It selects provider via get_llm_provider, obtains credentials through get_llm_api_key/get_llm_model, then calls provider-specific generate functions. The resulting structured understanding is saved by UnderstandingStorage.
runtime_flow.evidence: Routes call get_llm_provider/get_llm_api_key/get_llm_model and app.services.project_understanding_service.generate_understanding and understanding_storage.save_understanding. app.services.llm factory contains OpenAIProvider and AnthropicProvider.

runtime_flow.title: Build HTML documentation and store assets
runtime_flow.description: DocumentBuilder.build_html creates a complete developer documentation HTML file that includes runtime/context blocks and screenshots when provided. DocumentStorage.save_document stores the HTML and runtime assets; the document file can be fetched via GET /api/projects/{project_name}/documents/{document_id}/html.
runtime_flow.evidence: generate_project_document and update_project_document call document_builder.build_html and document_storage.save_document; get_project_document_html returns a FileResponse from document_storage.get_document_file.

runtime_flow.title: Optional runtime context screenshot analysis
runtime_flow.description: If the submitted runtime context contains screenshots or text, _analyze_runtime_context_with_llm can call runtime_context_analyzer.analyze with a vision-capable model to extract structured, non-hallucinated insights from screenshots combined with the project scan.
runtime_flow.evidence: sync_project_documentation_with_context calls _analyze_runtime_context_with_llm which calls runtime_context_analyzer.analyze(stored_scan=..., runtime_context=..., provider_name=..., api_key=..., model=...). The analyzer docstring mentions vision-capable LLM usage.

screenshot_insights.title: Streamlit UI running at port 8501
screenshot_insights.what_it_shows: A developer started the Streamlit UI using 'streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py' and the server bound to 0.0.0.0:8501 (Local URL http://localhost:8501).
screenshot_insights.why_it_matters: Confirms the project includes a Streamlit frontend that communicates with the backend and can submit runtime context blocks and screenshots (ui/streamlit_app.py functions). The Streamlit UI is the expected way to attach screenshots and context for sync-with-context flows.

screenshot_insights.title: Uvicorn FastAPI backend on port 8000 handling requests
screenshot_insights.what_it_shows: Console output from 'uvicorn app.main:app --reload' showing Uvicorn running on http://127.0.0.1:8000 and multiple successful GET requests for /api/projects/AutoDocX/documents with status 200.
screenshot_insights.why_it_matters: Confirms the FastAPI backend is running locally and serving the documented endpoints used by the UI (scan, sync, documents). The GET logs show document listing endpoints are reachable and returning data.

screenshot_insights.title: Auto-reload behavior when modifying routes
screenshot_insights.what_it_shows: Uvicorn/StatReload warnings and repeated 'Shutting down' / 'Started server process' messages triggered by changes in app/api/project_routes.py.
screenshot_insights.why_it_matters: Indicates development mode (--reload) is used. File changes trigger reloads which is helpful for iterative development but means the process will restart on edits; tests and long-running worker simulation should account for reload interruptions.

operational_notes: Backend API default observed at http://127.0.0.1:8000 (Uvicorn) — verify if a different host/port is configured via environment or run args.

Streamlit UI default observed at http://localhost:8501. Streamlit acts as a client that builds JSON + file multipart payloads and uses requests to call the API endpoints (see ui/streamlit_app.build_context_blocks_payload and sync_documentation_with_context).
Ensure required workspace directories exist or run create_required_directories() from app.core.config; code expects workspace/scans, workspace/understandings, workspace/documents.

Set environment variables required to call LLM providers. The code expects a provider name via LLM_PROVIDER and provider-specific API keys/models using names resolved in get_llm_api_key/get_llm_model (check your .env). Without valid keys the endpoints that call LLMs will fail (HTTP 502/500).
Uploads: use the sync-with-context endpoint to submit context blocks and screenshots. Streamlit UI already builds correct multipart payloads mapping screenshot_index in each block to uploaded files.
Generated HTML docs are stored locally and served via GET /api/projects/{project_name}/documents/{document_id}/html (FileResponse).

Developer workflow suggested by evidence: run uvicorn backend, run streamlit UI, open Streamlit, select project folder path, optionally paste screenshots/context, and press sync to produce/upgrade documentation.

risks_or_gaps.title: .env and LLM credentials missing or misconfigured
risks_or_gaps.description: The code requires LLM provider selection and API keys (get_llm_provider, get_llm_api_key). If LLM_PROVIDER or provider API keys are missing or wrong the understanding and runtime screenshot analysis endpoints will error. The repository scan does not show a sample .env or exact environment variable names in full, so developers must locate or create the .env and supply keys.

risks_or_gaps.title: Local filesystem storage scalability and backups
risks_or_gaps.description: DocumentStorage/ScanStorage/UnderstandingStorage save files to the local workspace directory. This is fine for local development but may not be appropriate for multi-user, long-term storage or CI; no remote object store configuration is visible in the scan.

risks_or_gaps.title: No authentication/authorization visible for API endpoints
risks_or_gaps.description: The project routes handle file uploads and LLM usage but the scan shows no authentication middleware or API key enforcement on endpoints. If deployed to a networked environment this could expose internal code metadata and LLM costs.

risks_or_gaps.title: Vision-capable LLM model selection and costs unclear
risks_or_gaps.description: Runtime screenshot analysis depends on a 'vision-capable' LLM model. The code references model selection but does not document which provider/model is required for vision features and what costs/latency to expect.

risks_or_gaps.title: Unclear behavior around deduplication and asset batching for screenshots
risks_or_gaps.description: Functions exist to merge/ dedupe screenshots and context blocks (_merge_screenshot_lists, _screenshot_signature). The exact rules and migration of asset directories (asset_batch_id, asset_directory) are not fully visible from the provided scan; this may need verification when uploading many screenshots or updating documents.

risks_or_gaps.title: Not visible from the provided context: Docker/Terraform/CI deployment
risks_or_gaps.description: User mentions Docker/Temporal in notes and context blocks, but the code scan and screenshots only show local Streamlit and Uvicorn runs. There is no visible Dockerfile, compose or deployment configuration in the provided scan, so containerized deployment is not confirmed.
